title: roupala monosperma var. ecuadorensis
intro: Roupala monosperma var. ecuadorensis, commonly known as Ecuadorian Roupala, is a plant species belonging to the family Cunoniaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Ecuador. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Roupala monosperma var. ecuadorensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including gastrointestinal issues and skin conditions.
